productionEvidenceSource: F0-002 Stage B, reconciled with a same-day task-supplied evidence snapshot (see F0-006_PRODUCTION_TOPOLOGY_EVIDENCE.md section 1)

processes.name: noramedi-api
processes.startCommand: npm run start (npx prisma generate && tsx src/index.ts)

processes.name: noramedi-worker
processes.startCommand: npm run start:worker (npx prisma generate && tsx src/worker.ts)

services.name: Nginx
services.role: TLS termination, reverse proxy, static asset serving (host-level, not containerized)

services.name: Redis
services.role: optional fail-open rate-limit counter store only

services.name: PM2
services.role: process manager, no ecosystem.config.* file in repository

configurationSources.note: Actual PM2 cwd for either process was never independently confirmed in production.

variables.name: RUN_BACKGROUND_JOBS
variables.default: unset = jobs run in API process

variables.name: CORS_ORIGINS
variables.note: server/.env.example defines this key twice; only the last assignment would take effect in a real .env file

deploymentGaps: No step reloads or restarts noramedi-worker
No step builds or publishes the frontend
No rollback subcommand or documented rollback procedure
Script is fail-fast (set -euo pipefail) but not atomic/transactional across the whole sequence

driftFindings.repositoryExpectation: CI declares Node 20; no engines field anywhere

driftFindings.productionObservation: Bare-VPS + PM2 + host Nginx; no Dockerfile/compose file exists in repository
noramedi-worker runs in production but no repository script reloads/restarts it

driftFindings.repositoryExpectation: Vite build script produces dist/ from source at a given commit
driftFindings.productionObservation: No repository script deploys the frontend build; served bundle vs source match unconfirmed

driftFindings.repositoryExpectation: backupService.ts comments describe PITR as a target capability

driftFindings.productionObservation: No durable evidence any restore has ever been exercised

driftFindings.repositoryExpectation: Deploy script assumes PM2 pre-installed, no privilege spec
backupService.ts hardcodes BACKUP_DIR on the same host, no offsite-upload code path
driftFindings.productionObservation: Backup directory local to the same VPS; no offsite copy evidence

risks.summary: Local patient/imaging file storage co-located with database on a single VPS, no S3-compatible object storage configured
Database backup directory local to the same VPS as the database; no offsite copy evidence
PITR unavailable (archive_mode=off); recovery granularity bounded by backup frequency (~11h gap observed)
Restore-test execution unverified — capability exists but no durable evidence of exercise
noramedi-worker has no repository-defined deploy/reload automation; update lifecycle undocumented

Potential duplicate background-job registration across API and worker when RUN_BACKGROUND_JOBS is not explicitly 'false'; duplicate execution mitigated by DB-backed JobLock, duplicate registration itself uncontrolled
Node.js version drift: production runs Node 22.23.1, only CI evidence pins Node 20, no engines field anywhere
PM2 processes run as root; no least-privilege separation
PM2 restart counts (14 API / 13 worker) at ~38-39 min observed uptime warrant operational review
Stale Docker Compose deployment runbook could mislead a future deploy attempt
Configuration source ambiguity: dotenv loads from process.cwd() with no repository-enforced working directory (no ecosystem.config.* file)

unverified: Literal value of RUN_BACKGROUND_JOBS in production
PM2 cwd for noramedi-api and noramedi-worker
Actual host Nginx configuration content (body limits, timeouts, headers, upstream directives)
Whether server/uploads/ content is included in any backup pipeline
Last restore test (remains UNVERIFIED, not proof it never happened)
